last_item: Band-1.5 CAPSTONE: wire the dormant telemetry sampling + alert evaluation loop into the server runtime (internal/server/background.go)
last_result: done — alerts finally fire in production. The whole alert subsystem was built+unit-tested but NEVER RAN (no caller of Collector.SampleOnce/Engine.Evaluate; scheduler never instantiated). Added internal/server/background.go: ListenAndServe→startBackgroundJobs(ctx) seeds alert.DefaultRules() idempotently (insert-missing-IDs only — never clobbers operator edits/disables), creates a scheduler.Scheduler, registers a 'telemetry-sample' job on cfg.Schedules.TelemetrySample (default @every 30s). runTelemetryCycle = Collector.SampleOnce (host/PG + backup health, buffers samples) → Engine.Evaluate(now) → dispatchAlertEvents → activeNotifiers (enabled stored Pushover/webhook channels, nil http client = timeout-bounded default). Collector+Engine built in newServer (OTLP exporter left nil; degrades gracefully + still buffers/evaluates). Loop tied to ctx (clean shutdown; in-flight notifier reqs ctx-bound so Stop returns promptly); startup seed bounded by 30s timeout. Refactored loadAlertChannels→loadAlertChannelsCtx for the request-less dispatcher. Tests: seed idempotency+preserves disabled rule; full cycle fires backup-failed and delivers 'firing' to an httptest webhook (+ state persisted firing); no-channel cycle is a clean no-op. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): bounded seed, default-branch warn for unknown channel kinds, documented ctx-cancellation shutdown. Full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build); web untouched.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) IN PROGRESS. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced (Dashboard + Backups banner). (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) ***CAPSTONE DONE: the telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP IS NOW WIRED (internal/server/background.go) — alerts fire in prod via the scheduler.*** ***NEW TOP 1.5 ITEM DISCOVERED: the scheduler was never instantiated in prod, so SCHEDULED BACKUPS HAVE NEVER RUN either — only POST /backups/run (manual button) makes a backup. Biggest data-loss gap now. The alert loop makes it LOUD (backup-stale/backup-failed/'no successful backup yet' will fire) but the fix is registering full/incremental/restore-test/digest jobs in background.go via s.sched.Register(name, cfg.Schedules.X, fn); empty spec disables. Watch overlap between full+incremental; verify Manager/Owner single-writer guard.*** Remaining 1.5 after that: nudge backups off-host (S3 default, warn local-only); restore-test surfacing (handleRestoreTest stub); optionally wire OTLP exporter (currently nil in the collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue (separate key namespace from telemetry/snapshot.go MetricKeys). Backups: internal/backup/manager.go writes terminal 'fail'/'success'; internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup). go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
